title: Humanism in a Posthumanist Age
gist: A Spring 2026 issue editor's framing argues that contemporary culture is swerving away from the humanist idealism that underpinned the modern liberal-democratic project, entering what it calls a posthumanist age.

insights.claim: Contemporary culture is undergoing a swerve away from the humanist idealism that historically informed the modern liberal-democratic project.
insights.speaker: Hedgehog Review
insights.so_what: Signals an intellectual-establishment read that the philosophical foundations of liberal democracy are eroding, a backdrop for how elite discourse frames legitimacy and institutions.
insights.quote: What we are witnessing today is a cultural swerve away from the informing humanist idealism of the modern liberal democratic project.

insights.claim: A leading cultural-criticism quarterly is devoting an entire themed issue to defending or reassessing humanism against posthumanist and antihumanist currents, treating the topic as a live civilizational stake rather than academic abstraction.
insights.speaker: Hedgehog Review
insights.so_what: Track which ideas magazines set the intellectual agenda; a whole issue on antihumanism suggests the theme is percolating toward broader elite consciousness.
insights.locator: Thematic: Humanism in a Posthumanist Age
